logisticRegression.labelSource: Training-ready workbook labels from docs/Sookta_Research_Training_Dataset_REBA_ISO11228.xlsx combined with document-guided ISO 11228-3 pseudo-label fallback
logisticRegression.iso11228LabelSource: docs/Sookta_Research_Training_Dataset_REBA_ISO11228.xlsx sheet ISO11228_Source_Labels rows with data_quality_flag=OK, exported to data/research/expert_labels/iso11228_training_ready_20260603.csv, plus ISO 11228-1/2/3 document-guided pseudo labels

xgboost.labelSource: Training-ready workbook labels from docs/Sookta_Research_Training_Dataset_REBA_ISO11228.xlsx combined with document-guided ISO 11228-3 pseudo-label fallback

requiredBeforeResearchTrainedAbTest: Expand expert labels to more media sessions before claiming a fully validated research-trained model.
Run on-device ONNX inference tests against fixed sample joint-feature vectors from the expert-labeled research dataset.
Calibrate the XGBoost probability thresholds again after additional low-risk and very-high-risk samples are labeled.
notes: The current Logistic Regression model is a real trained offline artifact using the 2026-06-03 training-ready workbook labels for all currently extracted pose rows, combined with ISO 11228 workbook labels and document-guided ISO 11228-3 pseudo labels where applicable.

The XGBoost ONNX model was retrained on 2026-06-03 from the refreshed labeled pose dataset. It accepts the canonical 51 raw MoveNet features so the current Flutter ONNX predictor can run it without a separate preprocessing layer.
It is suitable for research-prototype risk communication and repeated data collection, not for clinical diagnosis or a validated injury prediction claim.
